mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 8.0 percent, labor market deterioration triggers immediate equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in 70 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals synchronized consumer demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through earnings revision cycles.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day forward returns with a p-value below 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a contraction in the industrial production index signals capacity constraints and impending corporate margin compression, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases produce positive 20-day forward returns in over 65 percent of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above trend consistently reinforces corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week as markets price in sustained economic momentum.
failure_condition: GDPC1 values show zero predictive correlation with 5-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows, or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 220.0, inflationary pressures force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in 75 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL stays above 220.0, or the threshold effect vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment indicates robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS and UNRATE signal fails to generate positive 20-day forward returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a decline in retail sales reflects weakening consumer demand that directly reduces near-term corporate earnings forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines during tightening cycles yield positive 5-day forward returns in over 60 percent of instances, or the mechanism shows no directional consistency when FEDFUNDS is rising.
